evals.name: scaffold-new-site
evals.prompt: I'm launching a new site for my bookkeeping agency LedgerCraft (production domain will be https://ledgercraft.example, email hello@ledgercraft.example, contact form via Web3Forms at https://api.web3forms.com/submit). Set up an AI-ready Astro static site for it in ./ledgercraft — I want AI assistants and crawlers to be able to discover, quote, and act on the site. Replace any template placeholders with real values and real content (we do monthly bookkeeping, VAT filings, and payroll for small e-commerce companies in the Netherlands). Do NOT run npm install or a build — no network installs here; just tell me what remains unverified.
evals.expected_output: A ./ledgercraft project with the AIO template set: llms.txt/llms-full.txt, robots.txt with AI bot allows, openapi/mcp/ai-catalog/agents manifests, SeoHead/BaseLayout components, schema builders, validators; core $TOKENs replaced with LedgerCraft values and content tokens filled with real bookkeeping copy; honest note about unverified build.
assertions: llms.txt exists in the project public/ dir and mentions LedgerCraft
robots.txt explicitly allows GPTBot, ClaudeBot and PerplexityBot
agent action manifests (openapi.json and mcp.json) exist and parse as valid JSON
no unreplaced $TOKEN placeholders remain in any project file
content reflects the real business: bookkeeping/VAT/payroll/Netherlands appear in llms.txt or pages
build validator script shipped and wired into package.json (verify script)
summary honestly states the build/install was not run and lists unverified items

evals.name: retrofit-existing-site
evals.prompt: My existing Astro site is in ./site (BrightPath Consulting, will deploy at https://brightpath.example, email team@brightpath.example). Make it AI-ready — llms.txt, AI crawler permissions, structured data, whatever else matters, prioritized by impact. Keep my existing pages working and don't rewrite my content. Do NOT run npm install or a build; just make the changes and tell me what to verify afterwards.
evals.expected_output: Gap analysis, then grafted artifacts: llms.txt written from the actual BrightPath pages, robots.txt with AI bot allows, JSON-LD added to existing pages, semantic HTML fixes (h1, landmarks), agent manifests pointed at real content, validators wired into package.json. Existing pages preserved.
assertions: existing content preserved: about page still contains 'Founded in 2021' and index still has original service blurbs
llms.txt exists and reflects the actual BrightPath pages (mentions BrightPath and the about page)
robots.txt explicitly allows GPTBot and ClaudeBot
JSON-LD (application/ld+json) added to the homepage or a shared layout
sitemap wired (integration in astro.config or a sitemap file/route)
no unreplaced $TOKEN placeholders remain anywhere in the site
summary tells the user what to verify after npm install/build
agent-action manifests added (at least ai-catalog.json or mcp.json or agents.json) and parse as valid JSON
build validator script added and wired into package.json (a verify/validate script)

evals.name: audit-built-site
evals.prompt: Here's the built output of my sauna studio's website in ./dist (it deploys to https://nordwind.example). Audit it for AI readiness / AIO — how discoverable and usable is it for AI assistants and agents? Give me a scored report with the most impactful fixes first. Analysis only — don't modify the files.
evals.expected_output: A weighted scored report across the 10 AIO categories identifying: no llms.txt, robots.txt without AI bot allows, no sitemap, no JSON-LD, no h1 (h2 used), img without alt, div-soup instead of landmarks, JS onclick CTA without data-action, no canonical/meta description; prioritized fix list by category weight.
assertions: audit-report.md exists and contains a weighted category score table (8+ AIO categories with weights)
report identifies missing llms.txt
report identifies robots.txt lacking explicit AI bot allows
report identifies missing structured data / JSON-LD
report identifies semantic HTML problems (no h1 and/or img missing alt)
report has a prioritized fix list ordered by impact
dist/ files were not modified (identical to input fixture)
